rationale: Keep: the cited paper’s core finding is that consumers choose feature-rich options pre-use but experience lower post-use satisfaction (“feature fatigue”), which is directly aligned with the citing paper’s research question about why completed choices from larger sets can yield lower satisfaction. The citing note explicitly positions itself as extending prior “large-assortment downsides” to postchoice satisfaction mechanisms, making this cited work a plausible theoretical/comparison anchor rather than a generic mention. The reference is also an exact DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.2005.42.4.431).
